---
title: README
author: Kailin Xue
date: today
date-format: long
format: 
  pdf: default
  html: 
    self-contained: true
    df-print: tibble
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
execute: 
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r include=FALSE}
#| label: setup
pacman::p_load(tidyverse, targets, gt)
here::here()
```

STAT 4001 Statistical Consulting Project: Analyses and reports prepared for client Karl Berator.

## Project Overview

This project investigates the effect of three treatments (A, B, C) on two outcomes: **AF** (continuous) and **PR** (binary). The dataset (`FITFRfinaldatav3.xlsx`) contains 100 participants across 58 variables, including demographics, 51 SNP genotypes encoded under an additive genetic model, and the two outcome measures.

## Repository Structure

| Folder / File | Contents |
|---|---|
| `raw-data/` | Original Excel data file (`FITFRfinaldatav3.xlsx`) |
| `data/` | Cleaned dataset (`FITFR_clean.csv`) produced by the analysis script |
| `figs/` | All figures exported for reporting; `ppt_` prefix = PowerPoint-optimised exports |
| `R/` | Supporting R scripts |
| `tabs/` | Exported tables |
| `resources/` | Reference materials and other materials |
| `tests/` | Sanity checks and tests |
| `karl_data.Rmd` | Main analysis: cleaning, EDA, modelling, assumption checking; This is in folder `R` |
| `readme.qmd` | This file |

## How to Reproduce

1. Open `karls-analysis.Rproj` in RStudio.
2. Ensure required packages are installed — the script uses `pacman::p_load()` to load them automatically.
3. Knit `karl_data.Rmd` to reproduce the full analysis.

Cleaned data will be written to `data/FITFR_clean.csv`; figures will be saved to `figs/`.

## Key Packages

| Package | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `tidyverse` | Data wrangling and visualisation |
| `readxl` | Reading the raw Excel file |
| `skimr` | Quick distributional summaries |
| `inspectdf` | Column-type and frequency inspection |
| `naniar` | Missing value visualisation |
| `corrplot` | Correlation heatmaps |
| `tableone` | Stratified descriptive statistics (Table 1) |
| `patchwork` | Combining ggplot figures |
| `broom` | Model tidying — `augment()` for residual diagnostics |
| `car` | Variance Inflation Factors (`vif()`) |
| `ResourceSelection` | Hosmer-Lemeshow goodness-of-fit test for logistic regression |

## Analysis Summary

- **Data cleaning:** type coercion, sentinel value removal (`age = −99`), SNP additive encoding, zero-variance column flagging (`smoker`, `rs123456`)
- **EDA:** distribution plots, Table 1 by treatment, correlation matrix, SNP–outcome associations
- **Modelling:** linear regression for AF; logistic regression for PR; SNP candidates pre-screened via univariable regression (nominal p < 0.05); main-effects model selected for both outcomes
- **Assumption checking:** residual diagnostics and VIF for AF; Box-Tidwell, Hosmer-Lemeshow, VIF, and Cook's distance for PR
- **Key findings:** Treatment B produces the highest AF; age is the dominant predictor of both outcomes; rs19486 and rs91154 are the top SNP candidates for AF and PR respectively (exploratory only)
